---
title: "OGG... What kind of Git branch naming convention is commonly used??"
author: 
   - name: "Ryo Nakagami"
     url: https://github.com/RyoNakagami
date: "2025-09-14"
date-modified: "2025-09-16"
categories: [git branch]
---

## 🔎 Objectives

- Make branch purpose explicit, collaboration smoother, and automation possible.
- Create clarity, consistency, and traceability in how teams manage code.

## 🎯 Goals

- Define clear branch categories (feature, fix, release, etc.) with consistent tokens.
- Ensure branch names immediately communicate intent.
- Enable integration with issue trackers and CI/CD pipelines.
- Reduce confusion by standardizing naming across the team.

## 📘 Guideline

::::{.info-box}

:::{.info-box-title}

① Use lowercase letters and hyphens to separate words

:::

:::{.info-contents}

- Branch names should always be lowercase for consistency and avoid conflicts in case-sensitive file systems.
- Hyphens (`-`) make branch names easier to read than underscores or camelCase.  

:::
::::

::: {#exm- .custom_problem }
<br>

- ✅ Example: `feat/user-login`  
- ❌ Avoid: `Feat_UserLogin`, `FeatUserLogin`  

:::
---

::::{.info-box}

:::{.info-box-title}
② Start branch names with a clear token
:::

:::{.info-contents}
- Each branch should begin with a **category token** that defines its purpose.  
- Common tokens: `feat` (feature), `fix` (bug fix), `docs` (documentation).
- Use slashes `/` to separate tokens and descriptions 
- This makes the branch purpose obvious at a glance.  
:::
::::

::: {#exm- .custom_problem }
<br>

- ✅ Example: `fix/payment-timeout`  
- ❌ Avoid: `payment-timeout` (purpose unclear)  

:::

This clarity is not only helpful for humans reading the branch list or PRs but also allows Git commands and CI/CD scripts to filter, map, or operate on specific branch types efficiently.


```zsh
# Listing branches by token
git branch --list "feature/*"

# Pushing or mapping branches with tokens
git push origin 'refs/heads/feature/*'

# Deleting multiple branches by token
git branch -D $(git branch --list "feature/*")
```

---

::::{.info-box}

:::{.info-box-title}
③ Link to issues or tickets when possible
:::

:::{.info-contents}
- If your team uses Jira, GitHub Issues, or another tracker, include the ticket/issue ID.  
- This improves **traceability** between code, commits, and tasks.  
:::
::::

::: {#exm- .custom_problem }
<br>

- ✅ Example: `feat/123-user-auth`  
- ❌ Avoid: `feat/user-auth` (missing link to ticket)  

:::
---

::::{.info-box}

:::{.info-box-title}
④ Keep branch names short but descriptive
:::

:::{.info-contents}
- Balance **brevity** with **clarity**.  
- Avoid overly long names while still describing the intent.  
:::
::::


Long branch names can be very helpful when browsing a list of branches, as they provide more context. However, they can clutter one-line logs, obscuring the visible part of the log.

::: {#exm- .custom_problem }
<br>

- ✅ Example: `refactor/api-headers`  
- ❌ Too long: `refactor/update-the-way-we-handle-request-headers-in-api`  

:::
---

::::{.info-box}

:::{.info-box-title}
⑤ Maintain consistency across the team
:::

:::{.info-contents}
- All developers must follow the same convention.  
- Consistency improves collaboration and enables automation in CI/CD.  
- Document rules in `CONTRIBUTING.md` or your project wiki.  
:::
::::

::: {#exm- .custom_problem }
<br>

- ✅ Example: Everyone uses `feat/*` for features  
- ❌ Avoid: Mixing `feature/*`, `feat/*`, `new/*` (causes chaos)  

:::
---

### Branch Naming Convention

[Work Branches]{.mini-section}

| **Format**          | **Purpose / Goal**                         |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| `feature/<name>`    | New feature development                    |
| `bugfix/<name>`     | Bug fix                                    |
| `refactor/<name>`   | Restructuring code without behavior change |
| `docs/<name>`       | Documentation updates                      |
| `test/<name>`       | Testing-related work                       |
| `enh/<name>`        | Performance enhancements                   |
: {tbl-colwidths="[25,75]"}



[Lifecycle branches]{.mini-section}

| **Format**          | **Purpose / Goal**                         |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| `release/<version>` | Preparing a release                        |
| `hotfix/<name>`     | Urgent production fix                      |
| `sandbox/<name>`    | Experimental or prototype work             |
: {tbl-colwidths="[25,75]"}




References
----------
- [stackoverflow > What are some examples of commonly used practices for naming git branches?](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/273695/what-are-some-examples-of-commonly-used-practices-for-naming-git-branches)
